revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kubotsu_Ōji]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
'''窪津王子'''は熊野[[九十九王子]]の1番目。[[八軒屋船着場]]のすぐ近く、大阪市中央区天満橋付近にあった。現在その跡には坐摩神社行宮が建っている。また大阪市天王寺区堀越町の堀越神社の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」に合祀されている。
'''窪津王子'''は熊野[[九十九王子]]の1番目。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、大阪市中央区天満橋付近にあった。現在その跡には坐摩神社行宮が建っている。また大阪市天王寺区堀越町の堀越神社の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」に合祀されている。
'''窪津王子'''は熊野[[九十九王子]]の1番目。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、大阪市中央区天満橋付近にあった。現在その跡には坐摩神社行宮が建っている。また大阪市天王寺区堀越町の堀越神社の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」に合祀されている。 坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った上皇たちも立ち寄っている。

'''窪津王子'''（'''くぼつおうじ'''）は、熊野[[九十九王子]]の1番目。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]天満橋付近にあった。現在その跡には坐摩神社行宮が建っている。また大阪市天王寺区堀越町の堀越神社の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」に合祀されている。 坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った上皇たちも立ち寄っている。

[[坐摩神社]]はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った上皇たちも立ち寄っている。
== 最寄駅 == *坐摩神社行宮 [[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[大阪市営地下鉄谷町線]][[天満橋駅]] *坐摩神社 [[大阪市営地下鉄御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] *堀越神社 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] * [[渡辺津]]（窪津）

revision.text:
'''窪津王子'''（'''くぼつおうじ'''）は、熊野[[九十九王子]]の1番目。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]天満橋付近にあった。現在その跡には坐摩神社行宮が建っている。また大阪市天王寺区堀越町の[[堀越神社]]の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」に合祀されている。
[[坐摩神社]]はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った上皇たちも立ち寄っている。
== 最寄駅 == *坐摩神社行宮 [[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[Osaka Metro谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[天満橋駅]] *坐摩神社 [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線|地下鉄御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] *堀越神社 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] * [[渡辺津]]（窪津）
: '''窪津王子''' - [[坂口王子]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くほつおうし}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]]

'''窪津王子'''（'''くぼつおうじ'''）は、[[熊野権現|熊野]][[九十九王子]]の1番目の王子である。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[天満橋]]付近にあった。現在その跡には[[坐摩神社]]行宮が建っている。
後に[[四天王寺]]西門の近くに熊野神社として祀られるが、[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）2月10日に[[天王寺区]]堀越町の[[堀越神社]]の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」として移転された。
坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った[[上皇]]たちも立ち寄っている。
== 最寄駅 == *坐摩神社行宮 [[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[Osaka Metro谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[天満橋駅]] *坐摩神社 [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線|地下鉄御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] *堀越神社 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] * [[渡辺津]]（窪津）
: '''窪津王子''' - [[坂口王子]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くほつおうし}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]]

'''窪津王子'''（'''くぼつおうじ'''）は、[[熊野権現|熊野]][[九十九王子]]の1番目の王子である。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[天満橋]]付近にあった。現在その跡には[[坐摩神社]]行宮が建っている。
後に[[四天王寺]]西門の近くに熊野神社として祀られるが、[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）2月10日に[[天王寺区]]堀越町の[[堀越神社]]の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」として移転された。
坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った[[太上天皇|上皇]]たちも立ち寄っている。
== 最寄駅 == * 坐摩神社行宮 [[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[Osaka Metro谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[天満橋駅]] * 坐摩神社 [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線|地下鉄御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] * 堀越神社 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] * [[渡辺津]]（窪津）

revision.comment: Adding an intro to all of them. It has a magic word instead of a substitution because all of them are gonna be moved in the future to proper pagenames. See comment for more info.
revision.text:
''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group revision.text:
''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.comment: putting a progress marker in here
revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kujūku Ōji|Kubotsu Ouji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:100 shortest pages as of {{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{subst:CURRENTDAY}} {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}]]]] testing out the new category thing using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kujūku Ōji|Kubotsu Ouji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Kujūku Ōji|Kubotsu Ouji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]]

revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * **Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:** {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * **Ikasuri Shrine:** {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * **Horikoshi Shrine:** {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

revision.comment: fixing some common errors in [[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]]
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
