'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kubotsu Ouji}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]]
==Overview==

Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|九十九王子" to "|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji"
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子}}

[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:ubotsu Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}}== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915年}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}} == See Also! == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:ubotsu Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915th year }} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}} == See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:ubotsu Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915th year }} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}} == See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915th year }} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}} == See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

[[Category:11]] [[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] ]] [[ja:窪津王子]][[zh:窪津王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[sn:ubotsu Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubotsu Ōji]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

'''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''くぼつおうじ''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}'s {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine|ja|坐摩神社行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano Shrine|ja|熊野神社}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|ja|1915th year }} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町}}, {{ill|Tennoji-ku, Osaka|ja|天王寺区}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}} / {{ill|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|ja|Osaka Metro谷町線}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|ja|天満橋駅}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|ja|Osaka Metro御堂筋線}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|ja|本町駅}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} – {{ill|Tennoji Station|ja|天王寺駅}} == See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodo|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）}}

revision.text:
'''窪津王子'''（'''くぼつおうじ'''）は、[[熊野権現|熊野]][[九十九王子]]の1番目の王子である。[[八軒家船着場]]のすぐ近く、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[天満橋]]付近にあった。現在その跡には[[坐摩神社]]行宮が建っている。
後に[[四天王寺]]西門の近くに熊野神社として祀られるが、[[1915年]]（[[大正]]4年）2月10日に[[天王寺区]]堀越町の[[堀越神社]]の摂社・「熊野第一王子社」として移転された。
坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った[[太上天皇|上皇]]たちも立ち寄っている。
== 最寄駅 == * 坐摩神社行宮 [[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[Osaka Metro谷町線|地下鉄谷町線]][[天満橋駅]] * 坐摩神社 [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線|地下鉄御堂筋線]][[本町駅]] * 堀越神社 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[天王寺駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] * [[渡辺津]]（窪津）
: '''窪津王子''' - [[坂口王子]]

坐摩神社はもともとこの地にあったが、[[大阪城]]築城の際、大阪市中央区久太郎町に遷座した。熊野御幸を行った[[太上天皇|上皇]]たちも立ち寄っている。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|qq=|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|qq=|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|qq=|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|qq=|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|qq=|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|qq=|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|qq=|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|qq=|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|qq=|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|qq=|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|qq=|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|qq=|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]
