revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] / [[:en:Tanimachi Line|Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line]] – [[:en:Temmabashi Station|Temmabashi Station]] * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' [[:en:Midōsuji Line|Osaka Metro Midosuji Line]] – [[:en:Hommachi Station|Hommachi Station]] * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] – [[:en:Tennōji Station|Tennoji Station]]
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Today, the site is marked by the [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine]] near the western gate of [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]]. On February 10, [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, [[:en:Tennōji-ku, Osaka|Tennoji-ku, Osaka]], where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]] originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597673]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}}

interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11597673}} [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Horikoshi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
: '''Kubotsu Ōji''' – {{ill|Sakaguchi Ōji|ja|坂口王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaguchi Ōji|1=Sakaguchi Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425069}}

Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji}}. The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Q18989850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327|1=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station|1=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|1=Midōsuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station|1=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Q18989850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327|1=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Q18989850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327|1=Q135506327}} also visited this site.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|WD=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|WD=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|WD=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Q18989850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|WD=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|WD=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506327|1=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
== Nearest Stations == * '''Ikasuri Shrine Temporary Shrine:''' {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} / {{ill|Tanimachi Line|en|Tanimachi Line|WD=Q1142773|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} – {{ill|Temmabashi Station|en|Temmabashi Station|WD=Q1043084|lt=Temmabashi Station|1=Temmabashi Station}} * '''Ikasuri Shrine:''' {{ill|Midōsuji Line|en|Midōsuji Line|WD=Q1192413|lt=Osaka Metro Midosuji Line|1=Midōsuji Line}} – {{ill|Hommachi Station|en|Hommachi Station|WD=Q1134631|lt=Hommachi Station|1=Hommachi Station}} * '''Horikoshi Shrine:''' {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} – {{ill|Tennōji Station|en|Tennōji Station|WD=Q1132328|lt=Tennoji Station|1=Tennōji Station}}
== See Also == * [[Horikoshi Shrine]] * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Kiiji Route|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji Route|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kyuju-kū Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja|渡辺津（窪津）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe-tsu (Kubotsu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Ōji--><!--Kubotsu Ōji--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11597673}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:窪津王子]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}}} '''Kubotsu Ōji''' ('''窪津王子''') is the first of the {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|熊野九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}} shrines. It was located near the {{ill|Hachikenya Boat Dock|ja|八軒家船着場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachikenya Boat Dock|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenmabashi|ja|天満橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmabashi|1=Tenma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18215296}} area of {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Today, the site is marked by the {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|qid=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} {{nihongo|Temporary Shrine|行宮}}.
Later, Kubotsu Ōji was enshrined as {{ill|Kumano shrine|en|Kumano shrine|qid=Q3200355|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano shrine}} near the western gate of {{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}. On February 10, {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), it was relocated to {{ill|Horikoshi Shrine|ja|堀越神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Horikoshi Town|ja|堀越町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikoshi Town|1=Q18989850|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18989850}}, {{ill|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|en|Tennōji-ku, Osaka|qid=Q490592|lt=Tennoji-ku, Osaka|1=Tennōji-ku, Osaka}}, where it is now a subordinate shrine known as "Kumano Daiichi Ōji Shrine" (熊野第一王子社).
The {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|qid=Q11425667|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} originally stood at this location but was moved to {{ill|Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|ja|久太郎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūtarō-machi, Osaka|1=Kyūtarō-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60996434}} in Chūō-ku during the construction of {{ill|Osaka Castle|ja|大阪城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂城|1=Osaka Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q321242}}. Many retired emperors (*jōkō*) who undertook the {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野御幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506327|1=Q135506327}} also visited this site.
